summary: A co-related, synchronized timetable web app for exactly two users (User A and User B). Features: onboarding with Display Name + auto-detected/manual timezone selection, polling-based real-time private chat with image/file sharing (up to ~2MB), dual-column timezone-synced timetable (UTC storage, local display conversion, scrollable fixed-height container), Internet Identity authentication, admin reset panel (passcode: 161189) for clearing all data on demand, app-wide ambient music toggle, and a Sky Events Clock screen showing Sky Mean Time (Pacific Time/America/Los_Angeles), user's local time, and live countdown timers for Geyser/Grandma/Turtle events anchored to Pacific Time cycles. Each event lasts 10 minutes (not 7): Geyser starts at cycle_anchor, lasts 10 min; Grandma starts at cycle_anchor + 30 min, lasts 10 min; Turtle starts at cycle_anchor + 40 min. Built with React (Vite), Motoko backend on Internet Computer. App-wide Sky: Children of the Light visual theme with animated landing page sky background (golden-hour sky, drifting clouds, light particles), warm amber/soft indigo/pale gold celestial palette replacing the dark-teal theme across all screens.
architectural_notes: All timetable events and chat timestamps stored in UTC; frontend converts to viewer's local timezone for display
Real-time sync via periodic polling (no WebSockets); chat and timetable refresh every few seconds
Authentication via Internet Identity (passkeys, Google, Apple, Microsoft)
Backend: Motoko canister on Internet Computer; Frontend: React with Vite + TypeScript
Timezone handling via browser Intl API and date-fns-tz
File/media sharing capped at ~2MB due to canister storage constraints
Admin reset function protected by passcode 161189; clears all users, messages, and timetable events on demand from within the app
Recurring timetable tasks stored as a single recurring rule record (not duplicated per day); rendered on matching days by the frontend
Sky Events: each event duration is 10 minutes — Geyser at cycle_anchor, Grandma at cycle_anchor+30min, Turtle at cycle_anchor+40min (cycles every 2 hours anchored to 00:05 Pacific Time)
known_issues: Backend registration rejects new users as 'unauthorized' — registration must allow any unregistered principal to freely claim Slot A or B without admin approval; only block a third user once both slots are filled
Timetable entry creation shows 'scheduled entries not available in this version' — timetable add/edit/delete is broken and needs to be fixed

Admin reset panel returns 'only authorized admins can reset' even when correct passcode 161189 is entered — backend adminReset function has a principal-based auth check blocking valid passcode-only resets
Ambient music mute/unmute button is completely non-functional — no icon toggle and no audio plays when pressed
'Enter the Sky' button on the landing page is broken — clicking it does nothing and does not navigate into the app or trigger auth/onboarding flow
Post-auth navigation loop: after Internet Identity sign-in, user is redirected back to the landing page instead of being routed to onboarding or dashboard
App is stuck on loading spinner and won't open at all — runtime initialization issue in App.tsx, likely auth state check or backend query hanging/erroring on load and blocking the render

existing_features.title: Implement Internet Identity authentication. On first login, prompt the user to enter a Display Name and confirm their timezone (auto-detected via browser's Intl.DateTimeFormat API, with a manual override dropdown listing all IANA timezones). Store this profile data in the backend tied to the user's principal.

Implement a strict two-slot user system (User A and User B). The first principal to register occupies Slot A; the second occupies Slot B. No additional users can join. The dashboard must prominently display both users' display names and their current local times (auto-updating).

Build a polling-based real-time private chat between the two users. Poll for new messages every 3 seconds. Support text messages and file/image attachments up to 2MB. Images must render inline in the chat UI. Non-image files must appear as downloadable links. All message timestamps must be stored in UTC and displayed in each viewer's local timezone.

Build a dual-column, timezone-synced timetable view. The layout must have a 24-hour time axis on the left, with two side-by-side columns: 'My Schedule' and '[Partner's Name]'s Schedule'. All events must be stored in UTC. When rendered, each user's column must show events aligned to their own local time, so both users see a shared moment in time at the same row. Both users can add, edit, and delete tasks in their own column; changes must be visible to the other user within ~3 seconds via polling.

Apply a cohesive, modern visual theme across the entire app. Use a clean dark or deep-teal aesthetic with warm accent colors, sharp typography, and a card-based layout. Enforce the theme consistently across onboarding, dashboard, chat, and timetable screens.

On the Dashboard page, prominently display both users' display names and their current local times. Each user's clock card must auto-update every second to show live local time in their own timezone. If the partner slot is not yet filled, display a waiting/placeholder state in the partner clock card.

Wire the ChatInterface frontend component to the live backend. Replace any stubbed query/mutation hooks in useQueries.ts with real React Query hooks that call the backend's sendMessage command and getMessages query. Poll getMessages every 3 seconds. Display each message timestamp converted to the current viewer's local timezone.

Apply a full Sky: Children of the Light visual theme across the entire app, replacing the current dark-teal theme. The palette must use warm amber, soft indigo/violet, pale gold, and deep midnight-blue tones. UI elements should feature soft glows, celestial/starfield motifs, and ethereal card styles. Typography should feel light and luminous. Apply consistently across the landing page, onboarding modal, dashboard, chat, and timetable screens.

Implement an animated Sky: Children of the Light sky background on the LandingPage. The animation must render a golden-hour sky (deep indigo-to-amber gradient horizon) with: (1) procedurally drifting soft cloud layers, (2) subtle radial light-ray bursts near the horizon, and (3) slowly rising floating light particles (spirits/fireflies). The animation must run continuously via CSS or canvas and must not block interactivity.

Add an admin panel UI accessible from within the app (e.g., a hidden link or small admin icon in the layout/header). When opened, it must show a passcode entry field. If the user enters '161189' and submits, display a 'Reset All Data' button. Pressing the button calls the backend admin reset function and, on success, shows a confirmation message and reloads the app state.

Add a looping ambient relaxing audio track (Sky: Children of the Light style — soft, ethereal, gentle) that plays app-wide across all screens (landing page, onboarding modal, dashboard, chat, timetable). The audio must loop seamlessly and be managed via a single persistent audio instance that does not restart on navigation.

Add a persistent mute/unmute toggle button fixed on-screen (e.g., bottom-right corner) that controls the ambient music. The button must be visible on all screens at all times and must show a clear music-on / music-off icon. The user's preference (muted/unmuted) must persist across navigation within the session.

Fix the timetable entry creation bug that currently shows 'scheduled entries not available in this version'. The backend must expose working addEntry, updateEntry, and deleteEntry functions that any registered user can call to manage entries in their own timetable column. The frontend AddEditTaskModal must successfully call these functions and the TimetableView must reflect the changes within ~3 seconds via polling.

Update the AddEditTaskModal frontend component to include a recurrence selector UI. When creating or editing a timetable entry, users must be able to optionally choose between: no recurrence (default, one-time), every weekday (Mon–Fri), or every weekend (Sat–Sun). The selected recurrence pattern must be passed to the backend mutation when the form is submitted.

Update the TimetableView frontend component to render recurring timetable entries on all matching days. When the currently viewed day matches the recurrence pattern of an entry (weekday Mon–Fri or weekend Sat–Sun), the entry must appear in the timetable grid for that day as a positioned task block. On days that do not match the recurrence pattern, the entry must not appear. One-time entries continue to appear only on their specific date.

Add an editable display name UI to the Dashboard. Show the current user's display name with an inline edit control (e.g., pencil icon or 'Edit Name' button). When activated, display a small input field pre-filled with the current name and a Save button. On save, call the `updateDisplayName` backend function via a React Query mutation, invalidate the profile query, and reflect the updated name everywhere it appears in the app (clock cards, chat, timetable columns) without a full page reload.

Completely overhaul the ambient music system in `frontend/src/hooks/useAmbientMusic.ts`. The hook must: (1) create a single Audio element pointed at the ambient track source, (2) set it to loop, (3) attempt autoplay on mount and handle browser autoplay restrictions gracefully (start muted if blocked, unmute on first user interaction), (4) expose a `isMuted` boolean and a `toggle` function that correctly calls `audio.play()` or `audio.pause()` and flips the muted state, and (5) persist the muted/unmuted preference to sessionStorage so it survives navigation. The `MusicToggleButton` component must reflect the correct icon state (music-on vs music-off) based on `isMuted`.

Create a new SkyEventsScreen frontend page/component that displays real-time countdown timers for three recurring Sky: Children of the Light events. All three events cycle every 2 hours anchored to 00:05 UTC for Geyser. Use the following schedule per 2-hour cycle: Geyser starts at cycle_anchor (00:05, 02:05, 04:05, ... UTC) and lasts 7 minutes; Grandma starts 20 minutes after Geyser ends (i.e., cycle_anchor + 27 minutes); Turtle starts 5 minutes after Grandma ends (i.e., cycle_anchor + 32 minutes, assuming Grandma lasts a similar duration — use 7 minutes for Grandma too, making Turtle start at cycle_anchor + 34 minutes). Each event card must show: the event name, a live countdown (HH:MM:SS) to the next occurrence updating every second, and the UTC time of the next occurrence. Style the screen using the existing Sky: CotL celestial theme (amber/gold/indigo palette, glass card style, glows) consistent with the rest of the app.

Add a navigation button labeled 'Sky Events' (or an appropriate celestial icon + label) to the Dashboard navigation area, positioned immediately before the existing timetable navigation button. Clicking this button navigates to or reveals the SkyEventsScreen component.

Make the TimetableView component render inside a fixed-height scrollable container. The container height should be approximately 500–600px (large enough to comfortably show several hours of the 24-hour grid without being cramped) with overflow-y: auto so the user can scroll to see the full day. The timetable grid itself retains its full 24-hour height internally; only the outer container is capped and scrollable.

Overhaul the ambient music system in `frontend/src/hooks/useAmbientMusic.ts` to fix the non-functional mute/unmute toggle. The hook must: (1) maintain a single shared Audio element (singleton pattern) pointed at the ambient track source and set to loop; (2) on mount, attempt autoplay — if the browser blocks autoplay, start in a paused/muted state and attempt to play on the first user interaction with the document; (3) expose an `isMuted` boolean that reflects whether audio is currently paused/muted; (4) expose a `toggle` function that calls `audio.play()` when currently paused and `audio.pause()` when currently playing, then flips `isMuted`; (5) persist the muted/unmuted state to sessionStorage so it survives in-session navigation. Update `MusicToggleButton` to correctly show a music-on icon when `isMuted` is false and a music-off icon when `isMuted` is true.

Overhaul the SkyEventsScreen page (frontend/src/pages/SkyEventsScreen.tsx) layout to display, from top to bottom: (1) a large live 'Sky Mean Time' clock showing the current time in America/Los_Angeles (Pacific Time, respecting DST); (2) a prominent live 'Local Time' clock showing the user's local time; (3) an events table with three columns — 'Event Name', 'Next Event' (next occurrence time displayed in the user's local timezone), and 'Time to Next' (live HH:MM:SS countdown). The table must list exactly three events: Grandma, Turtle, and Geyser (in that display order, no Dreams Skater row). The existing Sky: CotL visual styling (amber/gold/indigo palette, glass card style, glows) must be preserved — only the data layout and content change.

Rewrite the useSkyEventCountdown hook (frontend/src/hooks/useSkyEventCountdown.ts) to anchor all event cycle calculations to America/Los_Angeles (Pacific Time) instead of UTC offsets. Each 2-hour cycle must be anchored to the equivalent of 00:05 Pacific Time. Within each cycle: Geyser starts at cycle_anchor (lasts 7 min); Grandma starts at cycle_anchor + 27 min (lasts 7 min); Turtle starts at cycle_anchor + 34 min. The hook must return, for each event: the next occurrence as a Date object (for local-time display) and the seconds remaining until that occurrence (for the HH:MM:SS countdown). Update every second via setInterval.

Update the `useSkyEventCountdown` hook in `frontend/src/hooks/useSkyEventCountdown.ts` so that each Sky: Children of the Light event lasts 10 minutes instead of 7. The updated cycle schedule per 2-hour block anchored to 00:05 Pacific Time must be: Geyser starts at cycle_anchor and lasts 10 minutes; Grandma starts at cycle_anchor + 30 minutes and lasts 10 minutes; Turtle starts at cycle_anchor + 40 minutes. All other logic (Pacific Time anchoring, per-event next-occurrence Date object, seconds-remaining countdown, 1-second setInterval updates) must remain unchanged.

Fix the 'Enter the Sky' button on the LandingPage (frontend/src/pages/LandingPage.tsx) so that clicking it correctly triggers the Internet Identity authentication flow and/or navigates the user into the app (showing the onboarding modal or dashboard as appropriate). The button must respond to click events and must not silently fail or do nothing.

Fix the post-authentication navigation loop in `frontend/src/App.tsx` and `frontend/src/pages/LandingPage.tsx`. After a successful Internet Identity sign-in, the app must NOT redirect the user back to the landing page. Instead, it must inspect the authenticated identity state and route the user to: (a) the ProfileSetupModal/onboarding flow if the principal has no registered profile in the backend, or (b) the Dashboard if the principal is already registered. The landing page must only be shown to unauthenticated (anonymous) users.

Audit and fix `frontend/src/App.tsx` so it never gets stuck in an indefinite loading/initializing state. The initialization flow must complete and render a visible UI within a bounded timeout regardless of backend query outcomes. Specifically: (1) add a hard timeout (e.g., 5 seconds) to the auth initialization check so that if `useInternetIdentity` does not resolve, the app falls back to showing the LandingPage rather than spinning forever; (2) ensure the backend profile-fetch query used to decide whether to show ProfileSetupModal or Dashboard does not block render — use React Query's `isLoading` vs `isFetching` states carefully and treat a query error as 'not registered' rather than a hanging state; (3) ensure `showLanding` is set to `true` whenever the initialization phase ends without a confirmed authenticated identity, so the LandingPage is always shown as the fallback.

Audit `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts` and any query hooks called during app initialization to ensure they have explicit `retry: 0` or low retry counts and defined `staleTime`/`gcTime` so a failed or slow backend call does not cause React Query to silently retry and block the UI indefinitely on first load.

new_feature_requests.summary: Onboarding screen with Display Name input and timezone auto-detection + manual override dropdown
Two-user system with exactly two slots (User A and User B), no friend search
Polling-based real-time private chat with image/file sharing up to ~2MB; images render inline, files as download links
Dual-column timezone-synced timetable: UTC storage, local display conversion, real-time edits visible to both users

Implement the strict two-slot user system on the backend: the first principal to register occupies Slot A, the second occupies Slot B, and no additional principals may register. Expose a query that returns both registered users' profiles (principal, display name, timezone) so the frontend can identify and display both users.

Sky: Children of the Light app-wide theme: animated golden-hour sky landing page with drifting clouds, light rays, and floating particles; warm amber/soft indigo/pale gold celestial palette across onboarding, dashboard, chat, and timetable; glowing UI cards and ethereal typography

Add backend support for the chat system: store messages as records containing sender principal, text content, optional file blob, optional file name, optional MIME type, and a UTC timestamp (nanoseconds). Expose: (1) a command to send a message, (2) a query to retrieve all messages, and (3) enforce that only the two registered users may send or read messages.
Add admin reset panel accessible from within the app, protected by passcode 161189, with a button to clear all backend data (registered users/slots, chat messages, timetable events) on demand.
Add app-wide ambient relaxing music (Sky: CotL style) playing across all screens (landing, onboarding, dashboard, chat, timetable) with a persistent toggle button (mute/unmute) fixed on-screen.
On deployment, wipe all backend state — clear all registered user slots, all chat messages, and all timetable events — so the app starts completely fresh with no existing data.

Add a backend admin reset function that accepts a passcode and, if the passcode matches '161189', clears all registered users, all chat messages, and all timetable events. The function must reject any call with a wrong or missing passcode.

Fix the backend registration logic in backend/main.mo so that any authenticated but unregistered principal can freely claim Slot A (if empty) or Slot B (if Slot A is taken but Slot B is empty), without requiring any admin approval or role assignment. Only reject a registration attempt when both Slot A and Slot B are already occupied. Remove or bypass any access-control check that treats new registrations as unauthorized.

Rewrite the `registerUser` function in `backend/main.mo` so that any authenticated (non-anonymous) principal that is not yet registered can freely claim Slot A if it is empty, or Slot B if Slot A is occupied but Slot B is empty. Remove or bypass any access-control check, role guard, or whitelist that currently causes new registrations to return an 'unauthorized' error. Only reject a registration attempt when both Slot A and Slot B are already occupied by different principals. An already-registered principal re-submitting their own profile should also be handled gracefully (e.g., return an 'already registered' variant rather than 'unauthorized').
Fix timetable entry creation — resolve the 'scheduled entries not available in this version' error so both users can add, edit, and delete tasks in their own timetable column.

Add recurring task support for timetable entries: users can optionally mark an entry as a recurring rule stored as a single record with a recurrence pattern (every weekday Mon–Fri, or every weekend Sat–Sun). The timetable view automatically renders it on all matching days without creating duplicate entries.

Extend the timetable entry data model in the backend to support an optional recurrence pattern field. The field must accept one of three values: none (one-time entry), 'weekday' (repeats Mon–Fri), or 'weekend' (repeats Sat–Sun). Store this as a single recurring rule record — do not create separate entries per matching day. The addEntry and updateEntry backend functions must accept and persist this field.

Fix admin reset function: remove principal-based authorization check so any caller who provides the correct passcode '161189' is permitted to reset all data, without being blocked by 'only authorized admins can reset' error.

Rewrite the `adminReset` function in `backend/main.mo` so that authorization is based solely on the passcode string. If the caller provides the correct passcode '161189', the function must clear all registered users, all chat messages, and all timetable entries and return a success result. Remove any principal-based or role-based authorization check that currently causes the function to return 'only authorized admins can reset' even when the correct passcode is supplied.
Allow users to edit/update their display name at any time from the dashboard or profile settings, saved to the backend and reflected everywhere in the app.

Fix the ambient music mute/unmute toggle button — it is completely non-functional (no icon toggle, no audio plays). Overhaul the audio system so the button correctly toggles its icon and ambient audio plays/pauses on each press.

Add a backend `updateDisplayName` function that accepts a new display name string from any authenticated, already-registered principal and updates that user's stored profile. The function must reject calls from unregistered or anonymous principals.

Add a Sky Events Clock screen accessible via a new button placed right before the timetable button. The screen shows real-time countdown timers for three recurring Sky: CotL events that cycle every 2 hours: Geyser (starts 00:05 UTC), Grandma (starts 20 mins after each Geyser ends), and Turtle (starts 5 mins after each Grandma ends). Each timer displays time until the next occurrence, updating live.
Make the timetable view scrollable with a fixed-height container so it takes less vertical space on screen without being too cramped or hard to read.

Overhaul Sky Events screen: show (1) Sky Mean Time = Pacific Time (America/Los_Angeles) as a live clock, (2) user's local time as a live clock, (3) events table with Grandma/Turtle/Geyser showing next occurrence in user's local time and live HH:MM:SS countdown. All event schedules anchored to Pacific Time. Remove Dreams Skater. Keep existing visual styling.
